OSPS-AC-01.01_justification:
The single maintainer's GitHub account has 2FA enabled (TOTP + hardware key). GitHub also enforces 2FA for every account touching this repo.

Evidence: https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/securing-your-account-with-two-factor-authentication-2fa
OSPS-AC-02.01_justification: GitHub's default: new collaborators inherit no repo permissions unless explicitly granted. This repo has no external collaborators — only the single maintainer.
OSPS-AC-03.01_justification:
Repository ruleset 17924860 enforces no direct commits and no non-fast-forward on the default branch. See Rules → Ruleset settings.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/rules
OSPS-AC-03.02_justification:
Same ruleset also prevents deletion of the default branch.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/rules
OSPS-AC-04.01_justification:
Every workflow declares an explicit least-privilege permissions: block (e.g. release.yml grants only contents/id-token/attestations/packages: write; ci.yml grants contents: read).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/tree/main/.github/workflows
OSPS-AC-04.02_justification:
Every actions/* invocation is pinned to a commit SHA with a trailing version comment; verified by OpenSSF Scorecard PinnedDependenciesID check (zero open alerts).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
OSPS-BR-01.01_justification:
Every release commit and merge commit is cryptographically signed (SSH ed25519). Ruleset requires signed commits.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/commits/main
OSPS-BR-01.03_justification:
Release artifacts (tarballs, .deb, .rpm) carry SLSA v1.0 provenance via actions/attest-build-provenance since v0.0.11. Verify: gh attestation verify <file> --repo sebastienrousseau/corral.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
OSPS-BR-01.04_justification:
The Docker image at ghcr.io/sebastienrousseau/corral is signed by cosign in keyless mode (OIDC identity). Verify: cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp='https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/.*' --certificate-oidc-issuer=https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com ghcr.io/sebastienrousseau/corral:0.0.11.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/pkgs/container/corral
OSPS-BR-02.01_justification:
Semantic Versioning: tags v0.0.7 → v0.0.11 follow semver 2.0.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases
OSPS-BR-02.02_justification:
Every release asset is tagged with the semver version in its filename (corralctl_0.0.11_linux_amd64.deb, etc.) and each is SLSA-attested + cosign-signed.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
OSPS-BR-03.01_justification:
Release build is fully automated via GoReleaser on tag push. See .github/workflows/release.yml.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml
OSPS-BR-03.02_justification:
Base Docker image is SHA-pinned (FROM alpine:3.20@sha256:d9e853…4b6bc). Every Actions dep in every workflow is SHA-pinned. Go module dependencies are lock-verified via go.sum.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/Dockerfile
OSPS-BR-04.01_justification:
All release automation runs in an ephemeral GitHub-hosted runner (fresh VM per job). No stateful build servers.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml
OSPS-BR-05.01_justification:
Release outputs are static: .tar.gz, .deb, .rpm, and an OCI image with byte-reproducible layers. GoReleaser's release.mode:replace guarantees deterministic artifact set per tag.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.goreleaser.yaml
OSPS-BR-06.01_justification:
CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog and semver. Every release since v0.0.7 has a dated section with Added/Changed/Fixed/Security groupings.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
OSPS-BR-07.01_justification:
gitleaks/dependency-review runs on every PR; goreleaser strips debug symbols; no secrets are ever committed (env vars used for HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN, AUR_KEY). Zero secret-scanning alerts.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/secret-scanning

Secrets policy: only three secrets used (GITHUB_TOKEN, HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN, AUR_KEY), all repo-scoped GitHub Secrets, rotated on personnel change (documented in SECURITY.md).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/actions
build_common_tools_justification: Uses Go toolchain (Go 1.26+) and GNU Make — both widely-installed, well-known FLOSS tools.
build_floss_tools_justification: Go, Make, GoReleaser, and all CI actions are FLOSS.
build_justification:
Makefile provides: build, test, test-race, vet, lint, format, clean. `.goreleaser.yaml` handles the release build.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/Makefile
contribution_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md documents fork/branch/PR flow, required verification (make test/build/format), signed-commit requirement, and coding standards.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md's 'Pull Request Checklist' section enumerates the acceptable-contribution criteria: make test passes, make build succeeds, README updated if behaviour changed, all commits signed. Coding standards documented separately.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
crypto_call_justification: Same as above — no crypto is invoked from application code.
crypto_floss_justification: No cryptographic libraries are directly used by the project; the transitive Go stdlib and go-github deps are all FLOSS.
crypto_keylength_justification: No user-facing crypto keys are managed by the project. The maintainer's SSH signing key is ed25519 (industry-recommended).
crypto_password_storage_justification: Project does not authenticate any users; no passwords are stored.
crypto_pfs_justification: No public-facing network service. HTTPS to github.com is a client-side TLS session where the server (github.com) chooses cipher suites; project has no configuration authority there.
crypto_published_justification: The project itself does not implement cryptography — it delegates to git (SSH/HTTPS), Go stdlib, and the sigstore/cosign toolchain. No home-rolled crypto.
crypto_random_justification: No cryptographic random values are generated by the project.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: Project does not implement or invoke cryptographic algorithms from application code. Transitive crypto (git SSH/HTTPS, sigstore, Go stdlib TLS) is maintained by their respective upstreams.
crypto_working_justification: No user-facing cryptography implemented by the project.
delivery_mitm_justification: Distribution is via HTTPS: github.com/releases, ghcr.io, Homebrew tap (HTTPS), AUR (HTTPS + signed PKGBUILD). No plain-HTTP paths.
delivery_unsigned_email_justification: The project does not use email as a distribution channel.
delivery_unsigned_justification:
All release artifacts have SHA-256 checksums in the auto-generated checksums.txt (goreleaser) + SLSA provenance since v0.0.11. Docker image is cosign-signed. Homebrew tap uses signed commits.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
description_good_justification: README.md's opening paragraph describes what Corral does: 'Automatically clone and organise GitHub repositories by visibility and language.' Follow-up sentences cover the MCP server angle.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues is the primary discussion channel — searchable, publicly viewable, no client install required. GitHub Discussions can be enabled if demand emerges.
documentation_basics_justification: README.md is the primary documentation: install, quick-start, features, architecture (mermaid diagram), interactive TUI, layout customisation, smart sync, exec mode, MCP server, usage & flags, examples, troubleshooting, FAQ.
documentation_interface_justification: GoDoc for every exported symbol (56/56 documented; 100% gate in CI via scripts/doc_coverage.go). Rendered at https://doc.corrallib.com. README's 'Usage & Flags' section documents every CLI flag; MCP tool/resource contracts documented in the MCP Server section.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Go does not have separate 'debug' vs 'release' builds with assertion toggles. Test suite runs unconditional assertions.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Race-detector findings must be fixed before merge (test failures block CI). Zero historical open findings.
dynamic_analysis_justification:
`go test -race` runs on every PR via CI — dynamic race-detector analysis of the test suite.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Project is written in Go; no memory-unsafe language present.
english_justification: README, CHANGELOG, SECURITY, CONTRIBUTING, SBOM, all issues, PRs, and code comments are in English.
enhancement_responses_justification:
Enhancement requests are triaged through the same Issues flow. Recent example: the MCP server proposal (PR #30) was scoped, agreed, implemented, and released in a single release cycle.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/pulls?q=is%3Apr+is%3Aclosed+enhancement
floss_license_justification: Released under the GNU General Public License v3.0 — a FLOSS license.
floss_license_osi_justification: GPL-3.0 is on the OSI-approved license list (https://opensource.org/licenses/GPL-3.0).
interact_justification: README's Install section documents Homebrew, AUR, .deb/.rpm, Docker, and source. The Troubleshooting and FAQ sections walk through common issues. GitHub Issues is the discussion channel.
know_common_errors_justification:
gosec, CodeQL, and Dependency Review run on every PR; zero open alerts across all three.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer has actively hardened the release path: SLSA provenance (v0.0.11), cosign keyless signing (v0.0.11), SHA-pinned base images and Actions (Scorecard PinnedDependencies alerts closed), and 100% SBOM coverage.
license_location_justification:
Full LICENSE file at repository root; SPDX identifier declared in .goreleaser.yaml and all downstream package configs.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/LICENSE
maintained_justification: The single maintainer is actively developing (v0.0.7 → v0.0.11 shipped within the last 30 days as of 2026-07-01). SECURITY.md commits to best-effort response within 7 days.
no_leaked_credentials_justification:
GitHub Push Protection is enabled on the repo. Zero secret-scanning alerts historically.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/secret-scanning
release_notes_justification:

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed VCS by design; the GitHub-hosted origin is only one of many possible clones.
repo_interim_justification: Every commit on main is a distinct addressable SHA; PR branches are preserved in the merge history. Any interim state can be checked out.
repo_public_justification: Public GitHub repository, readable without an account.
repo_track_justification: Git — a distributed VCS that tracks every change, its author, and the commit timestamp. Full history preserved.
report_archive_justification:
GitHub Issues preserves the full comment history of every issue, open or closed, indefinitely.

version_semver_justification: Follows Semantic Versioning 2.0 (semver.org). Documented in the CHANGELOG.md header.
version_tags_justification: Every released version has a signed Git tag (v0.0.7 → v0.0.11 all signed with the maintainer's SSH ed25519 key).
version_unique_justification: Every release has a unique semver tag (v0.0.7, v0.0.8, v0.0.9, v0.0.10, v0.0.11). Version is embedded in binaries via -ldflags at build time.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No critical vulnerabilities have been disclosed. If one were, the automated CI pipeline (Dependency Review + CodeQL + gosec) would catch it on the fix PR and Dependabot would open PRs for dep bumps.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat:
Zero known open vulnerabilities. Historic examples of fast fixes: alert #25 (Dockerfile pin) closed within hours of surfacing; the v0.0.7 non-interactive git env change closed a cron-hang class within one release cycle.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
vulnerability_report_private_justificati:
Private disclosure email in SECURITY.md; GitHub Security Advisories interface enabled at github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/advisories.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/advisories
vulnerability_report_process_justificati:
SECURITY.md documents the private channel: sebastian.rousseau@gmail.com, 90-day coordinated disclosure, and a best-effort 7-day initial-response commitment.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_response_justificat:
SECURITY.md commits to a best-effort initial response within 7 days of a private report.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
warnings_fixed_justification: No warnings in the current build. Any warning that surfaces would fail CI and block merge.
warnings_justification: `go vet ./...` runs on every PR (docs-check job + implicitly in `go test`). `golangci-lint run ./...` via `make lint`.
warnings_strict_justification:
`make lint` uses golangci-lint with default strict presets. `go vet ./...` fails on any diagnostic.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/Makefile
